series_description:
Calendars, appointment books or
programs and scheduling, or itinerary
records, purchased with state funds or
maintained by staff during business hours
that document appointments, itineraries,
and other activities of agency officials or
employees.
retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 1 year
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the calendars,
appointment, and itinerary records of elected
officials, executive staff, board or commission
members, division directors, and program heads
require archival review. Contact the State
Archives when these records have met their
retention periods.
CAUTION: A record of this type purchased with
personal funds, but used by a state official or
employee to document his or her work activities
may be a state record and subject to this
retention period. See Open Records Decision
635 issued in December 1995 by the Attorney
General.

series_description:
Master versions of all forms used
internally and externally by the agency,
including subsequent revisions to an
agency form or any associated design or
design modification requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_title:
Rules and Regulations,
Policies and Procedures -
Final
series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, rules, or similar
records distributed internally for the use
of employees or externally to the public or
those individuals or entities regulated by
an agency that sets out the rules, policies,
and procedures that govern an agency's
programs, services, or projects. Includes
working files and related documentation
used in creating rules, policies, and
procedures. Does not include
Administrative (APA) Rules (see L.20).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Working files and related
documentation used in creating the final rules,
policies, and procedures, are not subject to
archival requirement and may be disposed of at
the expiration of the retention period.

series_title:
Time Off and/or Sick Leave
Requests
series_description:
Requests and authorizations for
vacation, compensatory, sick,
Family and Medical Leave Act
(FMLA), sick pool leave, and other
types of authorized leave, and
supporting documentation. Includes email
requests or submission through CAPPS.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Schedules of prices charged by an agency
for services to the public or other
agencies, including any documentation
used to determine the charges.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Notes or text of speeches, papers,
presentations, or reports delivered in
conjunction with agency work.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only speeches, papers, and
presentations given by or on behalf of executive
staff, board or commission members, division
directors and program heads require archival
review.

series_description:
Any internally distributed manual,
guidelines, or similar records that establish
standard office procedures for the agency;
for example, agency style manuals,
telephone protocols, mail room
procedures, and departmental procedures
in the consumer protection, licensing and
legal areas.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description:
Any document that initiates, rescinds, or
amends a regulation, policy, or procedure
that governs the programs, services, or
projects of an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
The annual budget approved by the
Finance Commission.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description: Register compiled by mailroom staff.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Interagency Vouchers to Dep't of Banking,
Dep't of Savings and Mortgage Lending,
Dep't of Information Resources, etc.
Includes telecommunications billing detail
as well as summary detail, also includes
any accompanying detailed listing of long
distance calls.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
NOTE: The billing agency will maintain all long
distance TEX-AN records and will provide each
using agency its bill summary of centralized
capitol complex telephone service without call
detail records.

series_description:
Records of accounts deemed uncollectible,
including write-off authorizations and
returned checks, warrants, and drafts.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Records of clearing accounts that are used
to hold transactions for later posting and
to ensure information is recorded
correctly and complete.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Signed CTIA forms for all employees
having USAS and CAPPS Financials access
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Documentation relating to the capital and
fixed assets of a state agency, including
equipment or property history cards
containing data on initial cost,
depreciation schedules or summaries used
for capital outlay budgeting, and property
sale, auction, or disposal records of
agency owned equipment and property.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years

series_description:
Records documenting the internal and
external procedural requirements with
respect to the accounting department of a
state agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Bill copies or stubs, statements, billing
registers, account cards, deposit warrants,
cash receipts compiled by mailroom,
credit card receipts, receipts books, cash
transfers, daily cash reports,
reconciliations, general journal vouchers,
special vouchers, and similar records that
serve to document money owed to or
received by a state agency and its
collection or receipt.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Bill copies or stubs, statements, billing
registers, account cards, deposit warrants,
cash receipts, credit card receipts, receipts
books, cash transfers, daily cash reports,
reconciliations, general journal vouchers,
special vouchers, and similar records that
serve to document money owed to or
received by a state agency and its
collection or receipt. Also includes
purchase vouchers (requisitions, orders,
receiving reports, invoices, or statements,
change orders, best value
determinations).
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Signature cards or similar records
establishing authority of an agency
employee to initiate or authorize financial
transactions on behalf of an agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Bank statements, credit card statements,
cancelled checks, check registers, deposit
slips, debit and credit notices,
reconciliations, and other banking related
records.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Periodic reports compiled from
information entered into the Uniform
Statewide Accounting System (USAS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Yearly report compiled from information
entered into the Uniform Statewide
Accounting System (USAS).
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Requests and authorizations for travel;
participation in educational programs,
workshops, or college classes; or for other
work-related activities for which the
expenses of the employee are defrayed or
reimbursed.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
List of agency contracts, leases, and
agreements, including general obligation,
land lease, utilities, and construction
contracts.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Records documenting requests for
payment of a sum of money according to
the terms of a policy or contract.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Sufficient records to verify information on
unclaimed property previously reported to
the State Comptroller showing the name
and last known address of the apparent
owner of reportable unclaimed property, a
brief description of the property, and the
balance of each unclaimed account, if
appropriate.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years

series_description:
Workpapers relating to reports compiled
by agency personnel on a daily or other
periodic basis pertaining to workload
monitoring, task completion times,
number of public contacts, and similar
activities.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
NOTE: These are workpapers and transitory
information files. The final copy is the Finance
Commission Meeting book.

series_description:
Drafts of proposed legislation and related
correspondence.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Copies of all proposed, withdrawn,
emergency, and adopted rules; open
meetings notices; or any other documents
required by law to be submitted to the
Texas Register.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments: Includes Notice of Rate Ceilings.

series_description:
Logs or similar records listing records
destroyed or transferred to the Archives
and Information Services Division, Texas
State Library and Archives Commission,
showing records series title, dates of
records, and date destroyed or
transferred.
comments:
CAUTION: Disposition can mean destroyed or
transferred.

series_description:
Worksheets or working papers used to
capture records inventory information,
including location tracking and to
document existence and requirements of
a records series.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
Agency copy. Formerly RMD 105.
Includes documentation of certification
and approval - forms SLR 105C (formerly
RMD 105C), and/or other forms
designated by the State Records
Administrator.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
NOTE: Original is retained by the State and Local
Records Management Division, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.

series_description:
Forms used to track the transmittal of
records to/from onsite and offsite storage
areas or a transfer of physical custody.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Information resources and operational
strategic plans prepared in accordance
with 2054.095 and 2056.002, Government
Code.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The archival requirement is met
by sending the required copies of the plans to
the Texas State Publications Depository Program,
Texas State Library and Archives Commission.

series_description:
Charts or diagrams that show the
structure of an agency and the
relationships and relative ranks of its
departments and job positions.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only charts showing the overall
arrangement and administrative structure of the
state agency need to be transferred to the
Archives and Information Services Division, Texas
State Library and Archives Commission.
Organizational charts showing
division/department level detail are not
considered archival.

series_description:
Logs or similar records used to register
persons appearing before state agencies
as required by Chapter 2004, Government
Code, including quarterly reports filed
with the Texas Ethics Commission.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Agency level documents authorizing final
disposition of records under a certified
records retention schedule.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Minutes or notes, and supporting
documentation, taken at internal agency
staff meetings.

Biennial narrative reports to the governor
and legislature as required by an agency's
enabling statutes, including annual
narrative reports if they are required by
statute; includes the Report on
Availability, Quality and Pricing of Certain
Financial Services and Consumer Loan
Products and the SDSI Reports.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version of these
agency reports must be submitted to the Texas
State Publications Depository Program per 13
TAC 3.3. The requirement is met by sending the
required copies of the reports to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission. Working files
and related documentation used in creating the
final report may be disposed of at the expiration
of the retention period.

series_description:
Sunset Review Report, agency self-study,
and other correspondence and supporting
documentation related to the Sunset
review process for state agencies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Related documentation
includes the agency self-evaluation report,
agency response to the commission's draft
report, and documentation of the agency's
implementation of the commission's final
recommendations.

series_description:
This series documents state and federal
grant projects participated in or
administered by state agencies. It includes
grant authorization records, which provide
evidence of the award of grants to or by
agencies; grant/project financial or
performance reports, which are periodic
reports of financial activity and/or
program performance related to grants
received or made by agencies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: Retention requirements may vary
depending on the specific funding agency.
Agencies must ensure that records are retained
for the appropriate retention period.

series_description:
Non-awarded, denied, or unfunded grant
applications and proposals.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
For vehicles, equipment, etc. a) Executed,
renewed, or amended on or after
September 1, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Assessments or evaluations of the value of
state-owned buildings or property.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Archival review designation is
for state-owned buildings only. OCCC owns its
building.

series_description:
Records documenting building space
requests and approvals. Information on
request forms may include but is not
limited to: the building and room; the
reason for the change; parties involved in
the request; date of request and approval.

Equipment manuals and instructions along
with manufacturer's warranty information
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_title:
Building Construction
Contract and Inspection
Records
series_description:
Building construction contracts, surety
bonds, and inspection records.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 10 years
comments: SEE ALSO numbers 5.2.002 and 5.2.003a/b.

series_description:
Records management plans and similar
records that establish the policies and
procedures under which records and
information are managed in an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description:
Plans and records relating to the process
of planning new or redefined programs,
services, or projects of an agency that are
not included in or directly related to other
records series in this schedule.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Data processing planning
records are not archival.

series_description:
Documents submitted at meetings of state
boards, commissions, committees, and
councils, including exhibit items,
documentation for agenda items, public
comment forms, etc. Includes documents
sent in advance of meetings for briefing
purposes, some of which may not be
submitted at an actual meeting.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
Instructional materials and other records
developed by OCCC associated with
training consumers it serves, including but
not limited to training manuals, course
registration, class rosters, sign-in sheets,
syllabuses, course outlines, and similar
training aids used in external training
programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Includes news and press releases or public
relations files maintained or issued by an
agency.

Disaster Recovery Purposes. PhysIcally
remote, cloud-based backups are
captured every night between EOD and
next BOD, including new files, modified
files, and taking note of deleted files. Files
can be recovered through DIR STS service
request.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments: Backup copies are updated nightly.

series_description:
Records documenting operational and
maintenance requirements of computer
hardware such as operating manuals,
hardware/operating system requirements,
hardware configurations, and equipment
control systems, and associated
processing files.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for access to
electronic records must be retained for the
period of time required to access the records.

series_description: Old Internet Web Pages.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Automated software applications and
operating system files including job
control language, program listing/ALECS
source code, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for access to
electronic records must be retained for the
period of time required to access the records.

series_description:
Records adequate to specify all technical
characteristics necessary for reading or
processing electronic records; to
document modifications to computer
programs; to access, retrieve, manipulate,
and interpret data in an automated
system; and allow for their timely,
authorized disposition. Records include
but are not limited to: user guides, system
or sub-system definitions, system
specifications, input and output
specifications, system flow charts,
program flow charts, program
maintenance logs, change notices, data
element dictionaries, file layouts, code
books or tables, and other records that
explain the meaning, purpose, structure,
logical relationships, and origin of the data
elements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for access to
electronic records must be retained for the
period of time required to access the records.

series_description:
Agency receives nightly cloud backup
reports from DIR.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Files needed for electronic data audits
such as files or reports showing
transactions accepted, rejected,
suspended, and/or processed; history
files/tapes; records of on-line updates to
application files or security logs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_title:
Software Registrations,
Warranties and License
Agreements
series_description:
Records documenting the registration and
licensing of a software application to
activate the software for legal use by the
end users of a state agency, along with
warranties providing that the software will
perform in accordance with functional
specifications.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years

series_description:
Records relating to the issuance of keys,
identification cards, building passes,
passwords, signed statements or similar
instruments of access to agency or state
facilities, equipment or automated
systems, including CTIA forms and
acknowldgments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Records documenting the request for and
response to help desk tickets received by
divisions or units, such as information
technology.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Biennial information security plan for
protecting the security of the agency’s
information.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
Relatively long-lived computer files
containing organized and consistent sets
of complete and accurate electronic
records and associated processing files.
Examples include, but are not limited to,
data tables of relational databases used by
applications or computer programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Records management officers must be
certain before assigning the retention period of
AC to agency master files and application data
that the files do not fall under records series
listed elsewhere.

series_description:
Files, information, and ideas that
document a project anywhere from
conception to research to development to
execution and testing. Functional histories
can be valuable at any later time if the
project must be reviewed, revisited, or
upgraded.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Schedule of examiner work, duty, or
assignments.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 1 year

series_description:
Records documenting the response to
public inquiry requests.

Includes mail logs, overnight delivery log,
complaint tracking log, etc.

Aptitude or skills tests administered-
required of job applicants or of current
personnel to qualify for promotion or
transfer.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: One copy of each different test
(different in terms of either questions or
administration procedures) should be retained
for the period indicated.

series_title:
Knowledge and Skills Tests
(Completed and Graded
Test Papers)
series_description:
Aptitude and skills test papers of job
applicants or of current personnel taking a
test to qualify for promotion or transfer.

Instructional materials and other records
associated with in-house training of
examiners on job duties and skills.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 5 years

series_description:
Certificates of completion, transcripts, test
scores, or similar records documenting the
training, testing, or continuing education
achievements of an employee.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_title:
Regulated Industry
Quarterly and Annual
Reports
series_description:
Industry reports of activity required to be
submitted. Includes: quarterly and annual
reports from licensees; annual report
error notification letters; annual report
delinquency lists; and annual report data
validation spreadsheets.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Includes all documentation and
correspondence relating to records filed
under Finance Code 348.006 and 7 TAC
84.205 by or on behalf of a licensee.
Includes tracking records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
DCA filings under Finance Code 348.604
and 7 TAC 84.309. Includes final approved
DCAs, approval letters issued by agency,
and transmittals.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
DCA filings under Finance Code 348.604
and 7 TAC 84.309. Includes all
documentation (draft agreements,
correspondence, and filing logs) for
approved DCAs, except as described in
E.24 or E.26. Also includes all
documentation for denied or withdrawn
DCAs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_title:
Debt Cancellation
Agreements (DCA) -
Approved List and Log
series_description:
Includes list of approved DCAs posted on
agency website and internal log of
approved DCAs.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
Form submissions under Finance Code
341.502 and 7 TAC Chapters 84 and 90.
Includes final accepted forms, acceptance
letters issued by agency, and transmittals
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_title:
Plain Language Forms -
Correspondence and Filing
Logs
series_description:
Form submissions under Finance Code
341.502 and 7 TAC Chapters 84 and 90.
Includes all documentation (draft forms,
correspondence, and filing logs) for
accepted forms, except as described in
E.27 and E.29. Also includes
documentation for rejected, disapproved,
or withdrawn forms.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Includes list of accepted plain language
forms posted on agency website.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are furnished to the public
under Public Information Act (Chapter
552, Government Code). Includes
Certifications.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are excepted under the Public
Information Act (Chapter 552,
Government Code), including records
provided to the legislature under section
552.008.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Public Information Request and
Governmental Agency Request dockets.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 2 years

series_description:
Reports made to the Office of the
Attorney General (OAG)on an agency's
Public Information Act activities, per
Government Code, 552.010.

Includes license verifications and other
requests from government agencies.
Includes information provided under
information sharing agreements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Records of final decisions in the
adjudication of an administrative action.
Includes proposals for decision, final
orders, settlement agreements, closing
correspondence, and closing memos.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 12 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only records that lead to
substantial or statutory changes to the
operations of the agency and its policies and
procedures should be retained for archival
review. Contact the State Archives when these
records have met their retention periods.
CAUTION: These records may be maintained with
related information, including meeting notices,
proofs of publication, and meeting minutes, and
require a longer retention period.

series_description:
Records created by or on behalf of an
agency in anticipation of or in the
adjudication of a lawsuit in state or federal
court to which the agency is a party.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Cases that set legal precedent
or exhibit historical value will be evaluated by the
Archives and Information Services Division of the
Texas State Library and Archives Commission for
archival preservation.

series_description:
Records of final decisions in the
adjudication of a lawsuit in state or federal
court to which the agency is a party.
Includes final orders, settlement
agreements, closing correspondence and
closing memos.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 12 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Cases that set legal precedent
or exhibit historical value will be evaluated by the
Archives and Information Services Division of the
Texas State Library and Archives Commission for
archival preservation.

series_title:
Rules Adopted Through
Administrative Procedure
Act (APA) - Final
series_description:
Includes precomment drafts distributed to
stakeholders, stakeholder notices and
Advanced Notice of Proposed
Rulemakings, precomments received, rule
actions published on agency website
(proposals and adoptions), and official
comments received.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
Final administrative rules are published in the
Texas Register, which maintains the record copy
of the rule. Rule proposals, adoptions, and
official comments are included in the Finance
Commission's meeting materials, which are kept
permanently. The OCCC sees no administrative
value in maintaining precomment drafts,
stakeholder notices and Advanced Notice of
Proposed Rulemakings, and precomments for
more than 3 years. The OCCC will maintain its
version of these records for only 3 years after the
date the rule becomes effective.

series_title:
Rules Adopted Through
Administrative Procedure
Act (APA) - Working Files
series_description:
Includes rule drafts, internal staff notes
and edits, staff notes from stakeholder
and Finance Commission meetings and
rule tracking documents.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
The final rule (see L.20) memorializes the
decisions of the OCCC related to the effective
rule. The OCCC sees no administrative value in
maintaining the working files longer than the
final rule, and will maintain the working file for
only 3 years after the date the rule becomes
effective.

series_description:
Information sharing agreements executed
with other state and federal agencies
including any correspondence that was
exchanged in negotiating the information
sharing agreement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Includes subpoenas and Civil Investigative
Demands (CIDs) received in cases where
the agency is not a party to the litigation,
and all correspondence and
documentation relating to records
provided in response to subpoenas and
CIDs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Official interpretations are those approved
by Finance Commission under Finance
Code 14.108. Includes the request for an
official interpretation.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
Original retained permanently by OCCC. Archival
requirement to be met by providing a copy to the
State Archives.

series_description:
Working files used in drafting official
interpretations, including withdrawn or
rejected requests, comments on requests,
and tracking records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Advisory letters are those defined under 7
TAC 1.201. Advisory bulletins are those
addressed to a group of stakeholders.
Includes letters from staff other than legal
counsel.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
US = End of fiscal year after advisory letter or
bulletin is superseded by statute, rule, litigation,
or otherwise

series_title:
Advisory Letter and
Advisory Bulletin Working
Files
series_description:
Working files used in drafting advisory
letters and advisory bulletins, including
requests eliciting advisory letters and
tracking records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_title:
License and Registration
Applications - Businesses -

correct errors on leave accumulation, and
to close out leave accounts on separated
employees.
